preamble: The shift from the voiceless uvular stop [q] to the voiceless uvular fricative [χ] is a common form of spirantization (lenition). This process occurs when the complete closure at the uvula is replaced by a narrow constriction, frequently observed in intervocalic or syllable-final positions.

examples.note: Frequent realization of the uvular stop as a fricative in intervocalic or word-final positions.
Allophonic spirantization of the uvular stop before another consonant.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
